title: Rich Indulgent Polish Apple Pudding
description: A traditional Polish dessert featuring caramel sauce and herbal notes, made with low-glycemic ingredients and a nimono-simmer method.
instructions: In a medium saucepan, combine the sliced apples, almond milk, honey, and lemon juice, and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the apples are tender.
In a small bowl, sprinkle the gelatin over 1/4 cup of cold water and let it soften for 5 minutes.
Add the softened gelatin to the apple mixture and stir to combine.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the vanilla extract.
Let the mixture cool to room temperature, then cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or until chilled and set.
Just before serving, drizzle the caramel sauce over the top of the pudding.
Garnish with chopped fresh mint leaves and serve chilled.
